author: Sofia Martínez
title: Zero-Click Search: How to Win When Users Skip Links
description: 63% of searches end without a click. Learn how to capture these zero-click moments through answer boxes, local packs, and snippets.

sections.p: We've been optimizing for clicks for 20 years. Here's the problem: 63% of searches don't end in a click to any website—Google or Apple Maps answers directly in the SERP. For local service businesses especially, this is both a threat and an opportunity. If you're invisible in these zero-click positions, customers are finding your competitors or just phoning directly. But if you own the featured snippet, local pack, or Knowledge Panel for your service area, you win before anyone leaves Google.

The biggest zero-click real estate is the local pack. When a contractor searches "emergency plumber near me" or "dentist open now," Google shows 3 businesses with reviews, hours, and directions. That pack captures roughly 40% of all local searches and 90% of mobile zero-click searches. If your plumbing company or dental practice isn't in the top 3, you're invisible. We tracked a physical therapy clinic in Portland that went from #8 to #2 in their local pack just by adding 15 service-specific photos and updating their Google Business Profile hours. Within 60 days, calls increased 34%—and most came directly from the pack, not from clicks to their website.

Featured snippets capture another 10-15% of searches. These are the answer boxes Google shows above all results—"What does physical therapy cost," "How long does a roof replacement take," "Best time to book a cleaning service." Unlike local packs, snippets send traffic if the user clicks through, but many don't. A home services company we worked with ranked for "how much does gutter cleaning cost" with a snippet that directly answered the question in 3 sentences. Users saw the answer, called for a quote, and skipped the website entirely.

list: Local pack results: 40% of all local searches, dominates mobile
Featured snippets: 10-15% of informational queries, knowledge panels
Answer boxes: Google's direct responses, zero navigation needed
Google Reviews: Direct call and direction buttons, skip website
Google's AI Overviews: Summarized answers with less link traffic

sections.h2: Winning the Local Pack (The Biggest Win)

sections.p: Local pack rank is 60% Google Business Profile optimization, 30% on-site SEO, 10% off-site signals. Most businesses ignore the first one entirely. A lawn care company we worked with had buried Google Business Profile information—no address verification, no photos, hours only updated once in 2019. We spent 6 weeks fixing the profile: verified the address, added 40 high-quality service photos (before/after work), updated all service categories, added 12 FAQs directly in the profile ("Do you mulch in winter?"), and set up a review request system. Result: moved from position 7 to position 2 in the local pack within 8 weeks. Calls increased 58%, and 70% of those callers said they found them in the pack first, then clicked the website only to confirm.

Google Business Profile photos are the second-biggest factor after reviews. If you have 3 generic photos and your competitor has 50 professional before/afters, you lose. A home services electrical company in Denver we audited had only 2 dusty photos. We created a system: every completed job got 5 photos (installation process, finished work, crew, customer testimonial screenshot). Six months of photos put them in the top 3 photos seen in Google searches for "electrician Denver." Customers clicking those photos saw professionalism and variety, which directly increased their callback rate by 42%.

Featured snippets are often easier to win than top 10 rankings. Google pulls snippets from pages already ranking 1-50, usually from pages currently ranking 4-8. We identified that "how long does house painting take" was getting a featured snippet, and the current snippet was vague ("1-3 weeks"). A painting company's blog post said "A typical 2,000 sq ft house takes 3-5 days with our crew of 4, depending on prep work and number of coats." Specific, measurable, helpful. We optimized the paragraph slightly and added a semantic heading. Within 2 weeks, they owned the snippet. For 8 months, that snippet sent 300-400 monthly visitors, nearly all of whom called for estimates because they already had a clear answer.

sections.quote: Zero-click wins aren't about getting everyone to your website. They're about being the first answer. Your Google Business Profile, your review content, your FAQ—these are the new homepage.

list: 1. Audit your Google Business Profile: 40+ photos, all services listed, all FAQs answered, verified address, correct hours
2. Find snippet opportunities: Use Ahrefs or SEMrush to find queries your competitors own snippets for, targeting 50-100 word answers
3. Optimize for "near me" and local intent: Include service area in metadata, local reviews in schema, address in footer
4. Answer the 12 most common customer questions: Add to your Google Business Q&A and blog FAQ schema
5. Track zero-click wins: In Google Search Console, add a secondary filter for 'Devices' and 'Mobile'—these show zero-click rates separately
